text: Fever in adults is generally considered a temperature of 38°C (100.4°F) or higher. High fever is above 38.5°C. Causes can include infections, inflammation, or heat exhaustion.
Hypothermia occurs when body temperature drops below 35°C (95°F). It is a medical emergency that requires immediate warming and care.
Normal blood pressure is consistently below 120/80 mmHg. Elevated BP is 120-129 systolic and <80 diastolic. Regular exercise and low sodium intake help maintain healthy levels.
Oxygen saturation (SpO2) levels between 95% and 100% are considered normal. Levels below 90% are critical (hypoxemia) and may require supplemental oxygen.
A normal resting heart rate for adults ranges from 60 to 100 beats per minute. Tachycardia (>100 BPM) can be caused by stress, caffeine, or underlying heart conditions.
Symptoms of respiratory infection include cough, shortness of breath, and low SpO2 levels. If SpO2 is below 92%, consult a physician immediately.
